question: How many vacation days do full-time employees receive per year?
ground_truth: Full-time employees receive 15 vacation days per year, and part-time employees receive a prorated amount.
answer: Full-time employees receive 15 vacation days per year, accruing at 1.25 days per month [Source 1]. Part-time employees receive a prorated amount based on their scheduled weekly hours [Source 2].
contexts: Full-time employees are entitled to 15 days of paid vacation per year, accruing at 1.25 days per month.
Part-time employees receive vacation days on a prorated basis calculated from their scheduled weekly hours.

question: How many sick days are employees entitled to each year?
ground_truth: Employees are entitled to 10 paid sick days per year, which do not roll over.
answer: Employees are entitled to 10 paid sick days per calendar year [Source 1]. Unused sick days do not roll over into the following year [Source 2].
contexts: All regular employees receive 10 paid sick days per calendar year.
Sick leave does not carry over; any unused balance is forfeited at year end.

question: What is the company's remote work policy?
ground_truth: Employees may work remotely up to three days per week with manager approval.
answer: Employees may work remotely up to three days per week, subject to manager approval [Source 1]. Fully remote arrangements require department head sign-off and a signed remote work agreement [Source 2].
contexts: Under the hybrid policy, employees may work from home up to three days per week with their manager's approval.
Fully remote arrangements must be approved by the department head and documented in a signed remote work agreement.

question: How do I request parental leave?
ground_truth: Submit a parental leave request through the HR portal at least 30 days before the expected leave date.
answer: To request parental leave, submit a request through the HR portal at least 30 days before your expected leave start date [Source 1]. Eligible employees receive up to 12 weeks of paid parental leave [Source 2].
contexts: Parental leave requests must be submitted via the HR portal no later than 30 days before the intended start date.
Eligible employees are granted up to 12 weeks of paid parental leave following the birth or adoption of a child.

question: What is the probationary period for new employees?
ground_truth: New employees serve a 90-day probationary period.
answer: New employees serve a 90-day probationary period from their start date [Source 1]. A performance review is conducted at the end of probation to confirm continued employment [Source 2].
contexts: All new hires are subject to a 90-day probationary period beginning on their first day of employment.
At the conclusion of the probationary period, the manager conducts a review to confirm the employee's continued employment.

question: What is the process for resetting a forgotten work password?
ground_truth: Use the self-service portal at reset.company.com or contact the IT help desk if locked out.
answer: To reset a forgotten work password, visit the self-service portal at reset.company.com and verify your identity with multi-factor authentication [Source 1]. If your account is locked, contact the IT help desk at extension 4357 [Source 2].
contexts: Employees can reset forgotten passwords through the self-service portal at reset.company.com after completing multi-factor authentication.
If an account is locked out, employees should call the IT help desk at extension 4357 for assistance.

question: What software is approved for installation on company laptops?
ground_truth: Only software listed in the approved software catalog may be installed; other software requires an IT ticket.
answer: Only applications listed in the approved software catalog may be installed on company laptops [Source 1]. Installing any other software requires submitting an IT request ticket for security review [Source 2].
contexts: Employees may install only applications that appear in the company's approved software catalog.
Any software not in the catalog requires an IT request ticket and a security review before installation.

question: What is the policy on using personal devices for work?
ground_truth: Personal devices must be enrolled in mobile device management before accessing company data.
answer: Personal devices must be enrolled in the company's mobile device management (MDM) system before they can access company email or data [Source 1]. The MDM enforces encryption and remote wipe capabilities [Source 2].
contexts: Under the BYOD policy, personal devices must be enrolled in the company mobile device management system before accessing any company data or email.
The MDM solution enforces device encryption and allows IT to remotely wipe company data if a device is lost or stolen.

question: How often must employees change their network password?
ground_truth: Network passwords must be changed every 90 days and meet complexity requirements.
answer: Network passwords must be changed every 90 days [Source 1]. Each password must be at least 12 characters and include uppercase, lowercase, a number, and a symbol [Source 2].
contexts: Company policy requires that network passwords be changed at least once every 90 days.
Passwords must contain a minimum of 12 characters including uppercase and lowercase letters, at least one number, and one special character.

question: How do I report a suspected phishing email?
ground_truth: Forward suspected phishing emails to phishing@company.com and do not click any links.
answer: If you receive a suspected phishing email, do not click any links or open attachments and forward the message to phishing@company.com [Source 1]. The security team will investigate and notify you of the outcome [Source 2].
contexts: Suspected phishing emails should be forwarded to phishing@company.com without clicking links or opening attachments.
The information security team investigates reported phishing messages and follows up with the reporter on the results.

question: What does the code of conduct say about conflicts of interest?
ground_truth: Employees must disclose any actual or potential conflict of interest to their manager and the compliance team.
answer: Employees must promptly disclose any actual or potential conflict of interest to both their manager and the compliance team [Source 1]. Failure to disclose a conflict may result in disciplinary action [Source 2].
contexts: The code of conduct requires employees to disclose any actual or potential conflict of interest to their manager and the compliance department as soon as it arises.
Failure to disclose a known conflict of interest is a violation of the code of conduct and may lead to disciplinary action up to termination.

question: What is the company's policy on accepting gifts from vendors?
ground_truth: Employees may accept gifts valued under 50 dollars; anything higher must be reported and may need to be declined.
answer: Employees may accept unsolicited gifts from vendors only if they are valued under 50 dollars [Source 1]. Gifts exceeding that value must be reported to the compliance team and may need to be returned or declined [Source 2].
contexts: The anti-bribery policy permits employees to accept nominal vendor gifts valued at less than 50 dollars.
Any gift valued at 50 dollars or more must be reported to compliance, which determines whether it should be returned or declined.

question: What are the rules about social media and company confidentiality?
ground_truth: Employees must not disclose confidential company information on social media and should not speak on behalf of the company without authorization.
answer: Employees must never disclose confidential or proprietary company information on personal social media accounts [Source 1]. Employees may not represent themselves as speaking on behalf of the company without prior authorization from communications [Source 2].
contexts: The social media policy prohibits sharing confidential, proprietary, or non-public company information on any personal social media platform.
Employees must not claim to speak on behalf of the company on social media unless they have been authorized by the corporate communications team.

question: How do I report a workplace safety hazard?
ground_truth: Report safety hazards immediately to your supervisor or through the safety reporting form.
answer: Report any workplace safety hazard immediately to your supervisor or by submitting the online safety reporting form [Source 1]. Imminent dangers should also be reported to facilities for urgent action [Source 2].
contexts: Employees are required to report workplace safety hazards immediately to their supervisor or through the online safety reporting form.
Hazards that pose an imminent danger must additionally be reported to the facilities team so urgent corrective action can be taken.

question: What training is mandatory for all new hires?
ground_truth: New hires must complete security awareness, code of conduct, and anti-harassment training within their first 30 days.
answer: All new hires must complete security awareness training, code of conduct training, and anti-harassment training within their first 30 days of employment [Source 1]. Completion is tracked in the learning management system [Source 2].
contexts: New employees are required to complete security awareness, code of conduct, and anti-harassment training during their first 30 days.
Training completion for all mandatory courses is recorded and monitored in the company learning management system.

question: How do I enroll in the company health insurance plan?
ground_truth: Enroll through the benefits portal within 30 days of your start date or during open enrollment.
answer: You can enroll in the company health insurance plan through the benefits portal within 30 days of your start date [Source 1]. After that window, changes are only allowed during annual open enrollment or after a qualifying life event [Source 2].
contexts: New employees may enroll in the health insurance plan through the benefits portal within 30 days of their start date.
Outside the initial enrollment window, coverage changes are permitted only during open enrollment or following a qualifying life event such as marriage or the birth of a child.

question: How much does the company contribute to the 401(k) retirement plan?
ground_truth: The company matches 100 percent of employee contributions up to 4 percent of salary.
answer: The company matches 100 percent of employee 401(k) contributions up to 4 percent of eligible salary [Source 1]. Employer matching contributions vest after two years of service [Source 2].
contexts: The company provides a 401(k) match of 100 percent on employee contributions up to 4 percent of eligible salary.
Employer matching contributions become fully vested after the employee completes two years of service.

question: What dental and vision benefits are offered?
ground_truth: The company offers dental coverage including two annual cleanings and vision coverage including one annual eye exam.
answer: The dental plan covers two preventive cleanings per year and a portion of major procedures [Source 1]. The vision plan covers one annual eye exam and an allowance toward glasses or contact lenses [Source 2].
contexts: The dental benefit includes two covered preventive cleanings annually and partial coverage for major dental procedures.
The vision benefit covers one comprehensive eye exam per year plus an allowance applied toward eyeglasses or contact lenses.

question: Is there an employee assistance program available?
ground_truth: Yes, the employee assistance program offers free confidential counseling sessions to all employees.
answer: Yes, the company offers an Employee Assistance Program (EAP) that provides free and confidential counseling sessions to all employees and their household members [Source 1]. The EAP is available 24 hours a day by phone [Source 2].
contexts: The Employee Assistance Program provides employees and their household members with free, confidential counseling sessions.
EAP support is available 24 hours a day, seven days a week, through a dedicated confidential phone line.

question: What is the company's policy on overtime pay?
ground_truth: Non-exempt employees receive 1.5 times their regular rate for hours worked beyond 40 in a week.
answer: Non-exempt employees are paid overtime at 1.5 times their regular hourly rate for any hours worked beyond 40 in a workweek [Source 1]. Overtime must be approved in advance by a manager [Source 2].
contexts: Non-exempt employees earn overtime pay at one and one-half times their regular rate for hours worked over 40 in a single workweek.
All overtime must be authorized in advance by the employee's manager before the additional hours are worked.
